entry.title: BIP 54 active on Bitcoin Inquisition
entry.summary:
The dialogue revolves around the nuanced approach taken for sharing sensitive technical details concerning block validation times within the Bitcoin development community. The person in question has opted for a cautious dissemination of information, balancing between the need for public awareness and the risk of exposing exploitable vulnerabilities. This strategy is manifest in the decision to only share intricate details on a semi-private platform, specifically the Delving thread ([Delving Bitcoin](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/worst-block-validation-time-inquiry/711)), which is presumably accessible to a select group of experts. The premise behind this selective sharing is the assumption that these experts possess the requisite knowledge to understand and potentially address the issues without further broad dissemination that could enable malicious exploitation.
Quality assurance testing has been conducted to evaluate various techniques and their potential mitigations concerning block validation times. However, the individual acknowledges the inherent value in additional testing, albeit with an emphasis on fostering consensus within the community as the primary objective of any public demonstration. Such demonstrations are deemed necessary but are approached with caution to avoid divulging too much information that could lead to optimization of attacks for profit or disruption.
To strike this balance, a demonstration showcasing the possibility of creating slow blocks was shared publicly, without revealing the methods for achieving the most severe impacts. This approach is aimed at verifying the potential for crafting slow blocks without fully exposing the extent to which this vulnerability could be exploited. Feedback from the community remains a vital component of this process, indicating an openness to revising the strategy based on collective input from the involved stakeholders.
